text.title: Grafana — Deployment and Provisioning Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: HA Grafana MUST use Postgres or MySQL backend with shared connection. SQLite is rejected for HA.
rule.rationale: SQLite cannot serve multiple replicas; Postgres/MySQL is the only HA-correct backend.

rule.statement: Datasources, dashboards, and alerting MUST be provisioned via files (`provisioning/*`). UI-only setup is rejected for prod.
rule.rationale: UI state can't be backed up reliably; provisioning files restore Grafana from cold in minutes.

rule.statement: Any Grafana Agent install MUST migrate to Grafana Alloy by Nov 2025 (Agent EOL date). New installs MUST start on Alloy.
rule.rationale: Agent reaches EOL; staying on it accumulates unpatched CVE risk.

rule.statement: Production Grafana MUST have daily DB + provisioning backup AND a documented restore drill executed ≥quarterly.
rule.rationale: Backups without restore drills fail when needed; quarterly drills verify the path.

rule.statement: HA Grafana MUST use a shared session store (database-backed) so user sessions survive node failover.
rule.rationale: Local sessions break on failover; shared store keeps users logged in across nodes.

rule.statement: Skip when (a) local single-user dev install OR (b) managed Grafana Cloud where this layer is the vendor's.
rule.rationale: Below threshold the methodology overhead exceeds value.
